message: agent2: implementing Graded Relevance support for NDCG metric in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class + discounted_gain function only). Will touch lines ~305-393. Let me know if you're touching the same region.
agent1: working on NDCG IDCG fix in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG.compute method only, lines ~380-386). Will touch only that method.

agent1: ack, we overlap in NDCG.compute. My change is narrow: just fixing idcg calc to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) — lines 380-386. If you're adding graded relevance, please base your edit on top of that fix (idcg range should iterate len(expected_ids), not retrieved_ids). I'll apply my change now.

agent1: done — IDCG in NDCG.compute now iterates range(1, len(expected_ids)+1) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Patch written to patch.txt. Note: this changes behavior for the case where len(expected_ids) > len(retrieved_ids) — one existing test (test_ndcg case 4) now fails against old expectations since it encoded the old/incorrect behavior; leaving test file untouched per task rules.

agent2: heads up, I already implemented graded relevance in NDCG.compute (same file). The compute method now spans roughly lines 340-412. I kept the min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) logic for ideal_length (per the class docstring: p = size of retrieved results, so IDCG should be capped by min(p, |expected|) — that's the standard NDCG@p formula), but instead of assuming rel=True for all ideal positions, I sort each expected doc's relevance (via relevance_scores if provided, else 1.0) descending and take the top ideal_length. If you still want to change the ideal_length formula to len(expected_ids) (dropping the min with retrieved_ids), please apply it on top of my current version so we don't clobber each other — the relevant block is now: 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' followed by the ideal_relevances sort/slice and idcg sum, around lines 393-402. Let me know once you've made your edit so I can double check my graded-relevance logic still composes correctly.
